{"attempt_id":"20260503T024909-67788006","harness":"claude","input_tokens":16,"output_tokens":3026,"total_tokens":3042,"cost_usd":0.44921875000000006,"duration_ms":70779,"exit_code":0}

This bead requires benchmarking the axon backend against the JSONL backend on a 1100-bead corpus, but the axon backend has not yet been implemented. The implementing bead, ddx-95ec5ed5 ("Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"), is still status=open, and there is no axon_backend.go (or equivalent) in cli/internal/bead/ — the only Backend implementation present is backend_jsonl.go. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") are unsatisfiable without that backend. The bead's dependency DAG was not encoded: per the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) decomposition, this benchmark bead was created with an implicit dependency on ddx-95ec5ed5, but no `ddx bead dep add` edge was recorded, so the queue surfaced this bead as ready prematurely. Recommended follow-up: 1. ddx bead dep add ddx-781a15cf ddx-95ec5ed5 (record the missing edge so the queue stops surfacing this bead until the axon backend lands) 2. Land ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface refactor, in_progress) and ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon implementation) first. 3. Re-queue ddx-781a15cf; at that point the benchmark can be written meaningfully against both backends. Writing a partial benchmark (JSONL only) would satisfy neither AC2 nor AC3 and would mislead the parent epic's AC6 ("parity or better than JSONL") gate.

The axon bead backend is not yet implemented, so the benchmark this bead asks for cannot be written end-to-end. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") require an axon Backend implementation; none exists in cli/internal/bead/. Evidence checked: - `cli/internal/bead/axon/` does not exist (Glob). - No `BackendAxon` constant, no `axon.New*`, no `NewAxonBackend` references in cli/internal/bead (Grep). - `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` exposes only `BackendJSONL`, `BackendBD`, `BackendBR`. - TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md) is the design spec; it lists the bench (test plan §4) as a Beta gate. - Sibling beads under parent ddx-5d49b14e show the prerequisite work is still open: * ddx-8d747049 open — "axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client" * ddx-95ec5ed5 open — "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker" * ddx-743bc194 open — "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend" * ddx-6602559c open — "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" (duplicate of this bead) Follow-up: this bead should depend on the axon backend implementation beads (ddx-8d747049 and/or ddx-95ec5ed5) and become ready only once those close. Alternatively, dedupe with ddx-6602559c. A jsonl-only benchmark on the 1100-bead corpus could be filed as a separate bead if a baseline is wanted before axon lands, but the AC as written here cannot be satisfied without the axon backend.

The benchmark harness is now present at cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go and the bead package passes `go test ./internal/bead/...`, but the current axon backend still does not meet AC3's parity-or-better requirement on the measured queue ops. Final benchmark runs on the 1100-bead corpus show axon slower than JSONL for Ready, Blocked, and Show, so I cannot honestly close this bead as complete yet. done: - Added backend-specific benchmarks for Ready, Blocked, and Show against JSONL and Axon. - Built a 1100-bead corpus with active, closed, and archived records. - Verified `go test ./internal/bead/...` passes. blocker: - The axon backend implementation needs further performance work before the benchmark results can satisfy the bead's AC3 parity gate. follow_up: - Optimize the axon ReadAll / archive-read path and rerun `go test -run '^$' -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/bead/...` until the benchmark document can honestly record parity-or-better results.
